text: Frontier models can recover the exact governing law of a genuinely novel formal system from sparse observations, when the frame family is disclosed and tools are permitted.
At least one frontier model (GPT-5.5) can hypothesize the correct mathematical class of a hidden nonlinear law unaided by disclosure, and fit it exactly, when code is permitted.

The Evaluator Ceiling (narrowed 2026-07-07 after published red-team, docs/CEILING_REDTEAM.md): an authored, answer-keyed evaluation cannot seek out and certify frame construction that is truer than its author's own best frame, in any domain where 'truer than the author's frame' is a coherent event — i.e. real-world structure, not synthetic worlds whose sealed law is definitionally the truth. Prediction-grading softens but does not remove the bound: it can decline to penalize a predictively-equivalent alien frame, yet cannot design the discriminating experiment that would reward a superior one, because the author still selects the probes from their own conceivable family. Adversarial generation relocates rather than removes the bound, raising the effective ceiling to the maximum over participating generator minds and yielding a pairwise generative-dominance measurement, not an escape.
defeaters: a demonstration that an answer-keyed probe certified construction of a frame provably outside its author conceivable family
a formal proof that held-out predictive grading escapes the ceiling without importing the generator author frame family
a working counter-design accepted through this record own refutation channel
escape_hatches: reality-as-generator: registered futures graded by the world itself, where no party holds an answer key

adversarial generation: one mind constructs the sealed world, a different mind must discover it, the instrument referees mechanically — the test then scales with the generating frontier rather than with the evaluator (first instance: world-013)
process-grading: scoring the construction trajectory rather than a fixed endpoint (open design problem; not yet mechanically specified)
